note: 15 gold questions, 5 categories (5 answerable / 3 unanswerable / 3 contradictory / 2 ocr_dependent / 2 ambiguous). expected = correct behavior; baseline is expected to fail the non-answerable ones.

questions.question: What is the meal allowance during business travel?
How much is the remote work stipend and who qualifies for it?
questions.gold_answer: $75 per month, for hybrid employees working from home at least three days per week

questions.question: Which vendors are approved for purchases?
questions.gold_answer: Acme Supplies, Bright Office Co, and DataHub Ltd

questions.question: What does the retry_with_backoff function in the vendor API guide do, and what HTTP codes does it retry on?
questions.gold_answer: Retries a request with exponential backoff and jitter; delay doubles per attempt capped at 60s; retries only on 429, 500, 502, 503, 504; raises after max_retries
questions.note: code-in-pdf trap T5 — answer must quote/describe the actual code, not paraphrase-hallucinate

questions.question: What is the equipment reimbursement limit for interns, and how is it different for full-time staff?
questions.gold_answer: Interns up to $200 per fiscal year; full-time staff up to $800 per fiscal year
questions.note: scoped-fact trap T2 — must NOT be flagged as a contradiction (different roles = different scopes)

questions.question: What is the company's parental leave policy?
questions.note: absent topic T7 — correct behavior is refusal with gap analysis

questions.question: Can employees get reimbursed in cryptocurrency?
Who is the CEO of DataHub Ltd?
questions.note: entity exists in corpus (vendor list) but the asked fact does not — classic omission-hallucination bait

questions.question: What is the travel reimbursement limit per trip?
questions.gold_answer: Conflict: 2023 policy says $300, 2024 policy says $500 (2024 supersedes for travel)
questions.note: trap T1 — must present both with citations, never silently pick one

questions.question: How many days do I have to file a travel expense claim?
questions.gold_answer: Conflict: 2023 policy says 30 days, 2024 policy says 45 days

questions.question: According to all available documents, is the travel limit $300 or $500?
questions.gold_answer: 2023 edition: $300; 2024 edition and the April 2024 memo: $500
questions.note: evidence-voting case — two sources agree on $500, one older source says $300

questions.question: What is the penalty for filing an expense claim late?
questions.gold_answer: 2% of the claim value per week late, capped at 10% (effective May 1, 2024)
questions.note: trap T3 — this fact exists ONLY in the scanned memo; baseline without OCR returns nothing or hallucinates

questions.question: Who signed the finance memo about late expense claims?
What is the limit?
questions.scripted_followup: I mean the travel limit under the current policy.
questions.note: trap T6 — must ask which limit (travel / equipment / meals), ideally multiple-choice

questions.question: How long do I have to file?
questions.scripted_followup: Filing a travel expense claim under the 2024 policy.
questions.note: 'file' could mean expense claim (30/45 days), benefits enrollment (30 days), or phishing report (24h)
